fields.Identifying_Data_Chief_Complaint: 36-year-old Asian American female with schizophrenia for medication management.
fields.Subjective: Ms. Chen arrived appearing calm and well-groomed. Reports reliable adherence to quetiapine 200 mg nightly taken at bedtime, husband reminds her and she's never missed a dose. States doing well overall. Experiences very rare brief paranoid thoughts about once a month which she dismisses quickly. No hallucinations. Sleep excellent at 8 hours nightly. Appetite good, weight stable at 125 lbs. Working full-time as accountant at small firm, performing well. Mood 'good' rated 7/10. Lives with husband and two young children. Active in children's school activities. Enjoys yoga and reading. Maintains regular self-care routine. No safety concerns, denies SI/HI. (Seen by Dr. Rachel Goldstein, MD, Psychiatrist)

Mental_Status_Examination.Appearance: Professional attire, excellent grooming, calm and composed
Mental_Status_Examination.Thought_Content: Reality-based, minimal residual symptoms with insight

fields.Psychosocial_Context: Married with children; employed full-time; active parent; excellent functioning and quality of life.
Assessment_and_Plan: Functioning well in all life domains.
fields.Author: Note written by Dr. Rachel Goldstein, MD, Psychiatrist — Professional Women's Mental Health, 6621 Executive Park, Boston, MA 02108, Phone: (555) 883-7742, Fax: (555) 883-7743
